water_temp_read.read: Water temperature is in a favorable trout range.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.
fly_reasoning.zebra_midge: Small and natural for clear, pressured, technical conditions.
fly_reasoning.pheasant_tail: A reliable natural nymph when trout are feeding but cautious.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing on the Chattahoochee River is very good with water temperature ideal for trout activity. Recent stocking supports effective use of eggs and attractor nymphs alongside natural patterns.
generated_text.best_window: The best window is day 1 morning due to low sky cover, moderate humidity, minimal rain chance, and calm winds, providing favorable conditions for trout feeding and clear clean drifts.
generated_text.current_conditions: Water temperature is 58.6°F, flow is 768 cfs, gage height is 1.82 ft, and turbidity is low at 2.4 NTU, indicating clear water conditions.
generated_text.weather_setup: Current sky cover averages 22%, pressure is steady at 30.09 inHg with unknown trend, wind is light from the NE at 1.6 mph with gusts up to 10.4 mph, and rainfall is minimal with a 16% chance and no expected accumulation.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout are likely positioned near riffles, runs, seams, and structure, favoring comfortable feeding lanes close to cover during high light. Use smaller natural nymphs, midges, soft hackles, and stocked trout attractors like egg patterns and squirmy worms with an emphasis on clean drifts and balanced presentations. Caution is advised as pressure trend data is unavailable.
